title: baccharis rupicola subsp. orientalis
intro: Baccharis rupicola subsp. orientalis is a plant species that belongs to the Rosaceae family, commonly known as the mountain falseheath. It is native to South America and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and inflammation. The plant is rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, providing various health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and alleviating symptoms of bronchitis. Baccharis rupicola subsp. orientalis can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine.
